findings: Wrote 44 test assertions across 1 test file (tests/keel-state-describe.test.cjs) covering all 5 AC-ids.
AC-1: 9 tests (exit 0, story ID in stdout, Scope/Current phase/Halted/Idle/Started labels, Completed phases section, Gate events used line) — all verified meaningful via Option A stub.

AC-2: 3 tests (combined exit-1 + readManifest die() message guard, story ID in stderr, contrast test — valid story shows separator while missing story shows no stdout) — all verified meaningful via Option A stub; trivially-passing 'exit=1' test was tightened to also require the exact FAIL: prefix and absence of a raw stack trace.

AC-3: 6 tests (sub-hour Xm Ys pattern match, sub-hour does NOT use Xh Ym with non-empty stdout guard, multi-hour Xh Ym pattern match, floor-rounding '1h 2m' for 62 min, absent updated_at shows 'unknown', boundary exactly 60 min shows '1h 0m') — all verified meaningful via Option A stub.

AC-4: 4 tests (product-owner in completed phases, current_phase=5 shows tdd-red via AGENTS[4], halted=true shows WARNING/HALTED line, gate events N/M matches manifest values) — all verified meaningful via Option A stub.

AC-5: 22 regression tests pinning status --all and single-story status outputs (exit codes, JSON validity, array type, fleet count, field values, sort order, empty directory, single-story field schema) — correctly remain passing with stub because they test cmdStatus/cmdStatusAll which are independent of cmdDescribe.

Stub verification (Option A): inserted 'throw new Error(not implemented)' before readManifest call in cmdDescribe, ran tests — 23 tests failed (all AC-1 through AC-4), 21 passed (all AC-5). Restored implementation: 44/44 pass.

Trivially-passing test remediation: 3 tests identified as trivially passing with stub were rewritten — (1) AC-2 exit-1 check combined with FAIL: prefix assertion and stack-trace absence check; (2) AC-2 stdout-empty check replaced with contrast test requiring separator in valid-story output; (3) AC-3 negative pattern check strengthened with exit-0 and non-empty stdout guard.
Test file uses .cjs extension because package.json sets 'type: module' — identical pattern to scripts/test-keel-state.cjs.
Test harness: zero-dependency spawnSync subprocess runner with separate stdout/stderr capture — matches conventions of the existing engine test suite.
No production code files modified in this phase — only tests/keel-state-describe.test.cjs (new) and docs/test-plans/KEEL-103-test-map.md (new).
conventions.md checked (ADR-001 lock-free rule): describe is lock-free read-only — no test action conflicts with this rule.
lessons.md checked (L-1 path migration): no test hard-codes the old invocation path; ENGINE constant is computed from __dirname.
decisions: Used .cjs extension (not .js) because package.json has 'type: module' and the test uses require(); matches the precedent set by scripts/test-keel-state.cjs.

Chose Option A stub verification (inline throw before readManifest) rather than Option B (file deletion) because the function is embedded in the existing keel-state.cjs file — moving the entire file aside would break all other commands in the test suite.

AC-5 regression tests pass with the stub by design — they invoke status/status --all which are entirely independent of cmdDescribe. This is correct: their meaningfulness is that they would fail if cmdStatusAll or cmdStatus were accidentally modified, not if cmdDescribe is removed.
Separate stdout/stderr capture (not merged) so AC-2 can independently assert 'stdout empty' and 'stderr has FAIL: prefix' without ambiguity.

patchManifestUpdatedAt writes directly to the manifest file (bypassing the engine) to control the exact idle-time timestamp for AC-3 tests — the engine's writeManifest always stamps updated_at to now(), making it impossible to produce a controlled past timestamp through normal engine calls.

AC-4 halted test uses a dual strategy: attempts gate-budget exhaustion first; if the manifest is not halted after that (timing edge), patches manifest.halted=true directly — this makes the test deterministic regardless of gate-count arithmetic.
